title: Outlier-robust loss
body:
Sarika notices that Rohan didn’t listen to Spotify at all on several days. These low-valued outliers cause her original model to underpredict the minutes listened. She aims to design a custom loss function that helps address this issue.

Assume Sarika uses this model: $$y=	heta_0+	heta_1 x_1+	heta_2 x_2+	heta_3 x_3$$

Sarika wants a loss function that minimizes the effect of outliers on the fitted parameters. Which loss function is most robust to outliers?
A: $$ rac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}**{n}(y_i-\hat{y}_i)$$
B: $$ rac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}**{n}\left|y_i-\hat{y}_iight|$$
C: $$ rac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}**{n}(y_i-\hat{y}_i)**{2}$$
D: $$ rac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}**{n}(y_i-\hat{y}_i)**{4}$$
